symptom: Application Gateway health probe failure or backend unhealthy due to NSG blocking required management ports on AppGW subnet
rootCause: NSG on Application Gateway subnet missing required inbound port rules (65503-65534 for v1, 65200-65535 for v2) and AzureLoadBalancer tag
solution: Allow inbound traffic from GatewayManager service tag (recommended over opening all ports), allow outbound to Azure Cloud service tag, and allow AzureLoadBalancer traffic
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.3 How to configure the NSG with the Application.md

symptom: ARM template deployment fails for Application Gateway with Key Vault SSL certificate integration, identity value error
rootCause: Downloaded ARM template contains principalId and clientId from the managed identity section, which are auto-generated and cannot be reused in a new deployment
solution: Delete principalId and clientId fields from the identity section in the ARM template before deploying a new Application Gateway
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.5[NETVKB]How to deploy APPGW by template if SSL.md

symptom: Application Gateway V2 memory utilization keeps increasing to 100%, causing outage
rootCause: Nginx process in AppGW V2 instances has a memory leak consuming excessive memory
solution: Short-term: PG enables nginx watcher to auto-reload process at 40% memory usage. Long-term: PG enables queue size limit to reduce nginx memory consumption. Both require PG engagement. Check ApplicationGatewayFeatureFlag >= 32768 for short-term fix status.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.7 [NETVKB] AppGW V2 memory leaking issue.md

symptom: Application Gateway V2 redirect location drops non-standard HTTPS port (e.g. 8010), redirect goes to port 443 instead
rootCause: When hostname override is configured in HTTP settings, host header contains only domain name without port, causing backend's Location header in 302 response to omit the port
solution: Workaround 1: Use rewrite rule to restore port in Location header with pattern (https://).*\.domain\.com(.*)$ and value https://domain:{var_server_port}{http_resp_Location_2}. Workaround 2: Change HTTP setting hostname override to include port or disable override and configure custom probe separately.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.8[MCVKB] The Application Gateway response with e.md

symptom: Cannot add VMSS in peered VNET to Application Gateway backend pool via Azure Portal
rootCause: Azure Portal limitation: Portal requires VMSS to be in the same VNET as Application Gateway, does not support cross-VNET peering backend configuration
solution: Use Azure CLI: 1) Get backend pool ID with az network application-gateway show, 2) Add VMSS to pool with az vmss update --add virtualMachineProfile.networkProfile...applicationGatewayBackendAddressPools, 3) Update instances with az vmss update-instances
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.10 [MCVKB]  Using Application Gateway with VNET.md

symptom: WAF on Application Gateway returns 403 blocked by SQL injection CRS rules (942260, 942370) for legitimate requests containing URL-encoded JSON
rootCause: OWASP CRS rules with urlDecodeUni transform decode URL-encoded characters (e.g. %5B%7B becomes [{), causing JSON payload to match SQL injection regex patterns
solution: Analyze blocked request: 1) Check WAF firewall log for matched ruleIds, 2) Verify with regex101 against CRS rules source, 3) Apply urlDecodeUni transform to request content before regex matching, 4) Consider creating WAF exclusion rules for legitimate traffic patterns
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.11 [MCVKB]  Troubleshoot Web Application Firewal.md

symptom: BFD (Bidirectional Forwarding Detection) not working on ExpressRoute private peering for older circuits
rootCause: BFD over private peering is only enabled by default on MSEE side for ExpressRoute circuits created after August 1, 2018. Older circuits do not have BFD configured.
solution: For older circuits: disable the peering and re-enable it to activate BFD on MSEE side. Customer also needs to enable and configure BFD on their PE router. Note: disabling peering will shut down BGP session and cause connectivity loss during the change.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====2.ExpressRoute=======/2.5[NET] BFD over ER private peer & peering Reset.md

symptom: VM with NAT Gateway: need to understand how VFP rules change when NAT Gateway is associated with subnet
rootCause: When NAT Gateway is attached, the RouteTargetInternet_0 VFP rule changes to use NVGRE encap with source=node IP and destination=NAT Gateway VIP, while SLB_SNAT_RULE remains unchanged
solution: Check VFP rules or Process Tuples on the host: SLB_SNAT_RULE stays the same, but RouteTargetInternet_0 changes to routeencap type with NVGRE encap pointing to NAT GW VIP. Use this to confirm NAT Gateway is correctly applied to the VM's outbound traffic.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.11[MCVKB] VFP rule of VM with NAT Gateway.md

symptom: Cannot create VNET peering between VNETs in different Azure AD tenants via Azure Portal in Mooncake
rootCause: Azure Portal does not support cross-tenant VNET peering directly. Previously guest user invitation across tenants was also not supported in Azure China, but now it is.
solution: Use Azure CLI: 1) Add guest users across tenants, 2) Assign Network Contributor role to the guest user on each VNET, 3) Use 'az network vnet peering create --remote-vnet-id <full-resource-id>' from each tenant's context with 'az account set' to switch subscriptions
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.12 跨租户配置VNET Peering.md

symptom: Sharp traffic decrease on Azure public IP — customer sees sudden drop then recovery in inbound traffic
rootCause: Azure platform DDoS protection mechanism blocks traffic matching suspicious patterns: UDP protocol, multiple source IPs targeting single public IP, small packet size, very large volume (e.g. heartbeat packets from many clients)
solution: 1) Contact 21V-WAPHYNET@oe.21vianet.com with public IP and time range to verify if protection was triggered. 2) If confirmed legitimate traffic, request rule disable (warns: IP will lose DDoS protection). 3) Long-term: customer should distribute traffic across multiple destination IPs.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.6[NET]How to Identify Traffic Decrease Caused by.md

symptom: Load Balancer sends all traffic to only one backend VM during pressure test with Client<->PLB<->NVA<->ILB<->Backend topology
rootCause: 5-tuple hash algorithm limitation: when client IP is fixed (pressure test), Hash(IP) is constant. Combined with NVA IPs having complementary hash values, all flows converge to the same backend VM through hash calculation
solution: This is expected behavior in pressure test scenarios with fixed client IP. In production with varying client IPs, traffic distributes evenly. For testing: use multiple source IPs, or understand that hash-based distribution requires entropy in the 5-tuple fields.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======4.LB=======/4.3[NET] Why does load balancer load all the traff.md

symptom: P2S VPN client gets Error 798: A certificate could not be found that can be used with this Extensible Authentication Protocol
rootCause: Root and client certificates created with Windows SDK 7.0 store root cert in Personal store. Win8/2012 and newer require root cert in Trusted Root Certificate Authority store, but SDK 7.0 certs don't auto-install there.
solution: Use Windows SDK 7.1A or later to generate root and client certificates. Certs created with 7.1A will auto-install root cert to Trusted Root CA store on Win8+ clients when installing the client cert (.pfx).
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======5.VPN Gateway====/5.2[VPN] VPN FAQ.md

symptom: Dynamic VPN tunnel only allows traffic through one local subnet when PFS is misconfigured
rootCause: Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) mismatch between Azure VPN Gateway and customer VPN device causes only partial subnet connectivity
solution: Ensure PFS is either disabled on the customer VPN device OR enabled on both sides including the Azure Gateway. PFS setting must be consistent on both ends of the tunnel.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======5.VPN Gateway====/5.2[VPN] VPN FAQ.md

symptom: DNS resolution failure when multiple VMs in the same VNet share the same hostname - nslookup returns wrong IP or NXDOMAIN
rootCause: iDNS updates the A record to the IP of the last-started VM with that hostname. In-guest restart does NOT trigger DNS record update; only deallocate+start from portal updates the record. When the currently pointed VM is stopped, the record is deleted (NXDOMAIN) rather than failing over to another VM with the same hostname.
solution: Avoid assigning the same hostname to multiple VMs in the same VNet. If duplicate hostnames exist: 1) DNS record always points to the last portal-started VM, 2) In-guest restart will not reclaim the record, 3) Stopping the VM deletes the record entirely.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======7.DNS=======/7.2[NET]How iDNS Manages The DNS Record when Multi.md

symptom: DNS resolution failures from Azure VM - Cannot resolve host errors due to DNS forwarder throttling
rootCause: Azure DNS forwarder enforces per-VM limits: max 200 queries in-flight (sent to iDNS but no response yet) and 500 queries per second. When exceeded, queries are dropped by the InFlightFilter.
solution: 1) Distribute DNS query load across more VMs. 2) Use custom DNS server - queries bypass node-level forwarder limits. 3) Diagnose via Jarvis: compare QueriesSentUdp vs QueriesDroppedByInFlightFilterUdp using Node ID and Container ID.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======7.DNS=======/7.3 How to Troubleshoot DNS Forwarder Related Issu.md

symptom: Previously accessible website returns 403 Forbidden or ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR after 21v HTTPS auto-blocking enabled
rootCause: 21v ICP compliance: HTTPS auto-blocking blocks access to domains without ICP registration. Blocking identified by redirect URL containing 42.159.191.244:5672/lanyun.htm in packet capture.
solution: 1) Quick verify: capture packet, check for 403 with lanyun.htm. 2) Transfer to ICP team for 21v BC cases. 3) Customer needs ICP domain registration. Affects WebApp, Network services.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======9.Other=======/9.1 Unregistered domain is 403 forbidden according.md

symptom: Global Azure VMs or clients outside China cannot resolve Mooncake Traffic Manager domain (trafficmanager.cn) - returns NODATA
rootCause: Traffic Manager geographic routing only maps CN region to endpoint. DNS queries from non-CN recursive DNS servers receive NODATA response per design.
solution: 1) Add geographic mappings for other regions or use All (World) catch-all. 2) Traffic Manager uses recursive DNS server IP to determine region, not client IP. 3) Verify with China DNS server (e.g. 101.226.4.6) from global VM.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/====6.Traffic Manager===/6.1[NET]The global Azure VMs query the Mooncake tr.md

symptom: VPN tunnel IKEv2 rekey issues or confusion about SA lifetime mismatch between Azure VPN Gateway and customer device
rootCause: IKEv2 phase1 SA lifetime is local concept - mismatched values do NOT prevent tunnel formation. Peer with lower lifetime initiates rekey via CREATE_CHILD_SA at (lifetime/2 +/- jitter). Azure VPN GW uses fixed 28800s.
solution: SA lifetime mismatch is acceptable by design. If customer sets lower lifetime (e.g. 120s on Cisco CSR), their device always initiates rekey. Verify in WFPdiag logs for CREATE_CHILD_SA messages.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======5.VPN Gateway====/5.3[NET]Regarding IKEv2 phase1 SA lifetime.md

symptom: NSG Flow Log JSON files from Azure Storage are difficult to read - raw format lacks human-readable timestamps
rootCause: NSG Flow Logs stored in JSON with Unix timestamps, making manual analysis cumbersome
solution: Use NSGFlowLogFormatter.exe: place .json files in same directory, run tool, generates formatted_xxx.json with readable timestamps
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.10 [NSG] NSG Flow Log Formatting Tool.md

symptom: Need to find the correct PG team for routing network ICM tickets
rootCause: Network PG has many sub-teams (AzDNS, NRP, SLB, Gateway Manager, NDM, etc.) and the correct team mapping is not always obvious
solution: Query Kusto cluster Aznw (aznw.kusto.windows.net), database aznwcosmos, table DstsCertTeamMappings for current network team mappings. Note: this is deprecated info from 2018, may be outdated
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======9.Other=======/9.2[Deprecated][NET] Network team mapping.md

symptom: Unmanaged Service Fabric Cluster - Service Fabric Explorer is unreachable, telnet to port fails
rootCause: Unlike Managed SF Clusters, unmanaged SF clusters created via Portal do not automatically create NSG inbound security rules on the VNET, blocking access to SF Explorer ports
solution: Create 3 NSG inbound security rules (for SF Explorer, client connection, and application ports) and associate the NSG to the Service Fabric Cluster VNet subnet
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Dev=======/2.4 非托管Service Fabric Cluster无法打开 Explorer.md

symptom: Need to capture network trace on Cloud Service Extended Support or Azure VM without installing third-party tools
rootCause: Wireshark/tcpdump not available on the instance and cannot install software due to restrictions
solution: Use built-in netsh trace: 1) netsh trace start persistent=yes capture=yes tracefile=c:\temp\nettrace.etl 2) reproduce issue 3) netsh trace stop 4) download etl2pcapng from GitHub (v1.11.0) 5) convert: etl2pcapng.exe file.etl output.pcapng 6) analyze in Wireshark locally
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Dev=======/3.8 Collect network package without install tools.md

symptom: Function App in ILB ASE cannot resolve domain name and shows Azure Functions Runtime is unreachable
rootCause: ILB ASE requires manual DNS configuration; Function App needs WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER app setting and CORS configuration to work in internal-only network
solution: 1) Configure ILB ASE DNS per docs (docs.azure.cn/app-service/environment/create-ilb-ase#dns-configuration) 2) Add WEBSITE_DNS_SERVER app setting with DNS IP 3) Verify with nameresolver in Kudu 4) Add CORS entries: https://functions.ext.azure.cn and the ASE app URL. All testing must be done from inside the internal network
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/6.2 How to make Function App work in ILB ASE.md

symptom: Function App deployment slot returns Internal Server Error and DevOps deployment fails with SCM timeout when Function App uses VNET integration with Storage Account Firewall
rootCause: When Storage Account is secured by VNet firewall, creating a deployment slot generates a random WEBSITE_CONTENTSHARE name but the corresponding File Share is NOT automatically created in Azure Storage, causing the slot to fail to start
solution: Pre-create the File Share in Azure Storage before creating the deployment slot. Set WEBSITE_CONTENTSHARE to a predefined unique value for each slot (main app + each deployment slot). This is by-design per MS docs: secured storage accounts in VNet require manual share creation as part of automated deployment
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/6.5 Function App deployment slot is unavailable.md

symptom: Azure Container App default URL is inaccessible in Azure China (21V) - URL shows incomplete or cannot be resolved
rootCause: China region requires ICP domain registration; the default Container App URL (*.azurecontainerapps.dev.cn) is not ICP-registered, so it is blocked by design
solution: Two options: 1) Create Private DNS Zone with A record pointing Container App environment default domain to its static IP 2) Use custom domain with certificate + Private DNS Zone. For custom DNS servers, forward unresolved queries to 168.63.129.16. Do not block 168.63.129.16 in NSG/firewall. Ref: docs.azure.cn/container-apps/environment-custom-dns-suffix
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/=======Web=======/8.1 azure container app的纯内网环境如何进行域名和IP的解析.md

symptom: Cannot read HTTPS/TLS encrypted traffic in Wireshark when troubleshooting Application Gateway or other Azure services
rootCause: TLS traffic is encrypted; Wireshark cannot decode without session keys. If cipher suite uses ECDHE/DHE (Diffie-Hellman), even private key will not decrypt.
solution: 1) Set environment variable SSLKEYLOGFILE=C:\temp\sslkey.log to capture session keys. 2) In Wireshark Preferences > TLS > (Pre)-Master-Secret log filename, point to that file. 3) If cipher uses ECDHE/DHE, disable DH via Group Policy replacing with non-DH suites (TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_*). 4) gpupdate /force and restart.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.23 [NET]How to decrypt the TLS_SSL traffic in W.md

symptom: Cannot get correct outbound (OUT) traffic stats for vNIC when Accelerated Networking is enabled - Jarvis dashboard shows zero or incomplete data
rootCause: With Accelerated Networking enabled, data plane packets bypass the vmswitch via SR-IOV, so the traditional vmswitch-based Jarvis dashboard cannot capture OUT stats
solution: Switch to the GFT namespace Jarvis dashboard instead of the default vmswitch namespace. Use GFT metrics dashboard for accurate AccelNet traffic stats.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.1[NET]How to get the OUT stats of vNIC if Accel.md

symptom: Need to query Application Gateway historical VIP address and configuration for troubleshooting
rootCause: Application Gateway VIP and config changes are recorded in Kusto but not easily visible through Portal or ASC
solution: Query Kusto table ApplicationGatewaysExtendedHistory with GatewayId filter to get VirtualIPs, GatewayName, Config. Note: deprecated article, endpoint may have changed.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.1[Deprecated][NET]how to query APP GW history VI.md

symptom: AVD RDP Shortpath not established - session falls back to reverse connect transport instead of direct UDP connection
rootCause: Missing one or more required configurations on session host: 1) Registry key fUseUdpPortRedirector not set to 1 under HKLM SYSTEM CurrentControlSet Control Terminal Server WinStations, 2) Windows Firewall rule for UDP 3390 not created, 3) NSG on session host subnet not allowing inbound UDP 3390
solution: Configure all three: 1) Add registry DWORD fUseUdpPortRedirector=1 and UdpPortNumber=3390. 2) Create firewall rule: New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName Remote Desktop - Shortpath (UDP-In) -Action Allow -Protocol udp -LocalPort 3390 -Service TermService -Enabled:True. 3) Add NSG inbound rule allowing UDP 3390. 4) Verify with connection info dialog showing UDP protocol.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## Feature Verification/RDP shortpath.md

symptom: Cannot create NSG rule or Route Table entry with WindowsVirtualDesktop service tag via Azure Portal in Mooncake - tag not visible in Portal dropdown
rootCause: Azure Portal did not support WindowsVirtualDesktop service tag in dropdown until June 2022. Portal UI limitation for this specific service tag.
solution: Use Azure CLI: For NSG: az network nsg rule create -g RG --nsg-name NSG -n Rule --priority N --source-address-prefixes VirtualNetwork --destination-address-prefixes WindowsVirtualDesktop --destination-port-ranges * --direction Outbound --access Allow --protocol Tcp. For Route Table: az network route-table route create with --address-prefix WindowsVirtualDesktop. Note: Fixed in Portal since June 2022.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## How To/Route_Restrict network traffic using service tag.md

symptom: AVD session host system services (RDAgent, BITS) cannot connect through proxy; services running as LocalSystem or NetworkService fail to reach Azure endpoints while user-level browsing works fine
rootCause: Proxy settings configured at user level are not inherited by LocalSystem or NetworkService accounts. Windows system services use separate proxy configuration from the logged-in user.
solution: Use bitsadmin to set proxy for system accounts: (1) bitsadmin /util /setieproxy localsystem MANUAL_PROXY proxy:80 "<local>" (2) bitsadmin /util /setieproxy networkservice MANUAL_PROXY proxy:80 "<local>". Replace proxy:80 with actual proxy address/port. Use "<local>" to bypass local addresses, or NULL if no bypass needed. Verify with: bitsadmin /util /getieproxy localsystem
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AVD/## TS Workflow/Scenario/Proxy issue.md

symptom: Need to check ExpressRoute circuit traffic bandwidth/rate for troubleshooting - customer reports slow throughput or wants to verify utilization
rootCause: ExpressRoute traffic data is available in Kusto (aznw cluster) by joining InterfaceTable with SubinterfaceTable on DeviceName and InterfaceName
solution: Query aznw.kusto.windows.net/aznwanjnp: Join ExpressRoute.SubinterfaceTable (filter by ServiceKey) with InterfaceTable on DeviceName/InterfaceName. Calculate inTput = ifHCInOctets*8/(Interval*1024*1024) and outTput = ifHCOutOctets*8/(Interval*1024*1024) for Mbps. Summarize MbitsInPerSecond/MbitsOutPerSecond by PreciseTimeStamp, render timechart. Inbound = onprem to Azure, Outbound = Azure to onprem.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====2.ExpressRoute=======/2.2[Deprecated][Expressroute]How to check Expressr.md

symptom: Need to quickly check customer ExpressRoute circuit traffic information and bandwidth utilization via Jarvis dashboard
rootCause: ExpressRoute circuit traffic can be visualized through Jarvis dashboard by providing the NRP ResourceID
solution: Use Jarvis dashboard (jarvis-west) with NRP circuit ResourceID (starting from /subscriptions/). Circuit info obtainable from customer or circuit dump including: Service Key, Circuit Location, Primary/Secondary Device, Service Provider, STAG, Bandwidth, Billing Type, NRP Resource URI. Use the shared dashboard link with the NRP ResourceID override.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====2.ExpressRoute=======/2.1[Deprecated][NET]how to check the customer Expr.md

symptom: Customer complains about uneven load distribution across Application Gateway backend pool - some VMs receiving more traffic than others
rootCause: Need to verify actual load distribution with data. AppGW may show expected behavior depending on session affinity and connection patterns.
solution: Download ReqResp log from Jarvis, use APPGWAnalyzer.xlsx tool (from csssupportwiki AppGW page) to generate load distribution graphs across backend pool. If two lines overlap in the graph, load is balanced. Also check overall response status and current connections via Jarvis Response Status dashboard.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====1. AppGateway=======/1.4[Deprecated][NET][AppGW] Generate graphs to ide.md

symptom: ExpressRoute public peering deprecation in Mooncake - cannot create new public peering configurations after October 1, 2019
rootCause: Microsoft migrated ExpressRoute access to Azure PaaS services from public peering to Microsoft peering. Public peering APIs and new configuration creation blocked after Oct 1, 2019. Additionally, ExpressRoute for Office 365 is not recommended by O365 PG.
solution: 1) Existing public peering configs remain supported. 2) Migrate to Microsoft peering with route filters for PaaS access. 3) Microsoft peering does NOT support O365/Dynamics 365 in Azure China. 4) For O365 connectivity issues, use aka.ms/FixMyMeetings process instead of ExpressRoute.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=====2.ExpressRoute=======/2.3[Deprecated]ExpressRoute critical Change in Moo.md

symptom: Global VNet peering constraint: Basic Internal Load Balancer frontend IP unreachable from globally peered VNet
rootCause: Original Azure design restriction: Basic ILB only worked within same region. After VNet upgrade, this restriction was lifted - both Basic and Standard ILB now work across global VNet peering.
solution: After VNet upgrade, Basic ILB frontend IP IS reachable across global VNet peering. If customer still experiences issue, check if VNet upgraded. Chinese docs may still show old restriction - refer to English docs for updated info.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.1[Deprecated][VNet] The constraints of globally.md

symptom: Global VNet peering creation fails in Portal when enabling Allow gateway transit and Use remote gateway simultaneously during peering setup
rootCause: Portal limitation during preview phase: enabling gateway transit options at peering creation time caused errors. The global VNet peering must be established first.
solution: Workaround: 1) Create global VNet peering without checking Allow gateway transit or Use remote gateway. 2) Wait for peering status Connected on both sides. 3) Then separately enable Allow gateway transit on hub VNet and Use remote gateway on spoke VNet. Verified working across all 4 China regions.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.2[Deprecated] Global VNET peering--gateway trans.md

symptom: Azure VM cannot send email via SMTP port 25 directly to external email providers - outbound port 25 blocked
rootCause: For Pay-As-You-Go and trial subscriptions created after November 15, 2017, Azure applies technical restrictions blocking direct outbound SMTP (port 25) from VMs to prevent spam/fraud.
solution: 1) Submit port unblock request via Fraud check page: https://deephorizon.trafficmanager.net/#/PortUnblock. 2) If Forbidden error, request access to azfraudportunblock MyAccess group (24-48h sync). 3) Fraud team investigation takes 3-4 business days. 4) Alternative: use authenticated SMTP relay service.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.5[Deprecated][NET][SMTP25]How to request access.md

symptom: VM network connectivity lost - need to check VFP and NMAgent interface health state for troubleshooting
rootCause: VFP or NMAgent health degradation on the host can cause VM network unavailability. The interface state needs to be verified from the host perspective.
solution: Query Kusto aznw.kusto.windows.net, database aznwcosmos, table NetworkVMAvailabilityCompleteV4_CAS. Filter by NodeId and ContainerId with time range. Project: NMAgentHealth, NMAgentHealthDetails, VFPHealth, VFPHealthDetails to identify if VFP was down and the reason.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.8[Deprecated][VFP_NMagent] How to check the inte.md

symptom: Packet loss suspected within Azure host - need to capture and parse host-level packet traces to identify where packets are dropped (vmswitch, VFP, NIC)
rootCause: Packets can be dropped at different layers: physical NIC, vmswitch, or VFP. Standard guest-level capture cannot identify which host component drops packets.
solution: Host capture cmd: netsh trace start provider=Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Vmswitch provider=Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Vfpext capture=yes persistent=yes tracefile=c:\temp\packets.etl PacketTruncateBytes=300 maxSize=2048 capturetype=both. capturetype: Physical (NIC), VMSwitch (switch extension), Both (combined). Update NetMon parser - add case 0x6558:Ethernet WNV to gre.npl. Example: ICMP drop with Insufficient Resource in vmswitch = receive buffer exhaustion.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/3.9 [Deprecated][NET] How to parse host packet cap.md

symptom: VM fails to obtain IP via DHCP after start - DHCPDiscover sent but no DHCPOffer received, network service fails with 100% packet loss
rootCause: NMAgent programming delay due to SwitchPortNotPresent error. WCF channel between NSM and RNM hits MessageSecurityException, causing channel teardown and reconnection with exponential backoff, resulting in >1 minute NM programming delay.
solution: 1) Check NMAgent programming in Kusto: DCMNMAgentProgrammingDurationEtwTable filtered by Tenant+MAC - look for ProgrammingError SwitchPortNotPresent. 2) Check MessageSecurityException: DCMNMRegionalNetworkConfigurationFailureEtwTable. 3) NSM 14.3+ has fix: alternative RNM endpoint. 4) Short-term: VM may recover after NM programming completes.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======3.vNet=======/[MCVKB] Azure DHCP workflow/ICM.md

symptom: Need to find the Physical Address (PA) of a Standard Internal Load Balancer for troubleshooting - old basic ILB method no longer works
rootCause: Standard ILB uses a public IPv4 address as PA (by design), different from Basic ILB. This enables global VNet peering without IPv6 GRE encap - Standard ILB traffic uses IPv4 GRE encap while regular VM cross-peering traffic uses IPv6 GRE encap.
solution: Use FcShell: 1) Find backend pool instance cluster/container-id. 2) get-fabric, get-container, Invoke-FclApi NetworkManagementService GetVnetAddressMappingForCAFromNmAgent with Node.Id, Container.Id, ILB-CA-IP. PhysicalIPAddress field = PA. Alternative: check VFP rules SLB_DNAT_RULE counter line which includes PA address.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======4.LB=======/4.1[Deprecated][NET]How to get the PA address of S.md

symptom: Need to verify Standard ILB backend pool VM health probe status - cannot determine if VMs are healthy or unhealthy from portal alone
rootCause: Standard ILB health probe results require checking SLB platform-level data which is not directly visible in Azure Portal for internal troubleshooting
solution: Get ILB PA address first (via FcShell or VFP rules). Then use Jarvis dashboards: SLBv2 Standard: jarvis-west share/26AA1173 (up/down history, packets, bytes, SNAT connections). SLBv1 Basic: share/C7CAD2E. Health probe status: share/C84B891D. Replace account (slbv2chinaeast/slbhpchinanorth/etc) and VipAddress parameter with ILB PA.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======4.LB=======/4.2[Deprecated][NET]How to check the Standard ILB.md

symptom: VPN Gateway V3 diagnostic IkeV3Gateway.etl file cannot be automatically parsed to readable text - IKEv3Gateway.txt file is empty or malformed
rootCause: Known ACIS packaging bug: the IKEv3Gateway.txt file is not generated properly in GatewayV3 diagnostic zip. GatewayV3 replaced WFPDiag.etl/txt with IkeV3Gateway.etl/txt. GatewayV3 identified by GatewayImplementationType AzureV3VpnGateway in GatewayHealthFiveMinuteTable.
solution: 1) Get brkike.man file. 2) Run: tracerpt IkeV3Gateway.etl -of XML -import brkike.man -o tempIkeV3Gateway.txt -y. 3) Parse XML to text with PowerShell to extract ProcessID, ThreadID, SystemTime, Message fields into IkeV3Gateway2.txt. Alternative: check IKELogsTable under BrkGWT in Jarvis for IKE errors.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/======5.VPN Gateway====/5.1[Deprecated][NET]How to convert the Azure VPN g.md

symptom: Need to verify node-level network latency using pingmesh data for Azure VM connectivity troubleshooting
rootCause: Azure PingMesh continuously measures server-to-server latency across the datacenter. Data available via Kusto (1-minute interval) and Jarvis (5-minute interval). Common confusion: unit is microseconds NOT milliseconds.
solution: Kusto: cluster netperf.kusto.windows.net, database NetPerfKustoDB, table AzPingMeshServerStatus. Filter by serverIP (NodeIp) and cluster name. Key metrics: avgPayloadRTTInMicroseconds. Use: summarize percentile(avgPayloadRTTInMicroseconds, 90) by bin(timestamp, 1min) | render timechart. Jarvis alternative: dashboard A2807BE3, supports filtering by vnetid, SrcCA, DestCA, containerid.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.10[Deprecated][NET][pingmesh]how to verify ping.md

symptom: Need to access host-level logs (NMAgent, SLBHP, RDAgent, host upgrade) for Azure VM networking troubleshooting
rootCause: Host logs are not accessible through standard Azure portal or guest OS tools. Requires AP XTS (Autopilot XTS) tool which can browse host file system.
solution: Use AP XTS: 1) Install on corp machine or use VSAW (pre-loaded). 2) Find nodeId/hostname/cluster via Kusto: azurecm.chinanorth2 LogContainerSnapshot filtered by subscriptionId/roleInstanceName, then LogNodeSnapshot for hostname. 3) Connect in XTS using NodeID/HostName/NodeIP (MSFTVPN required). 4) Log paths: Data/Logs/NMAgent, Data/slbhp/launcher, Data/RDAgentUpdate, Data/VmphuSvc/Logs/windows. For new clusters (ChinaNorth3): may need manual route addition for MSFTVPN-Manual gateway.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.12[Deprecated][NET]AP XTS (download host logs).md

symptom: Need to capture packets at physical node or TOR switch level in Mooncake using Everflow - requires JIT Jumpbox access procedure
rootCause: Mooncake Everflow portal requires CME account access through Escort-type JIT session. MS engineers only have AME accounts, so Escort maps AME to CME permissions via 21V engineer assistance.
solution: Step 1 JIT: Submit JIT request with active ICM, select Escort type (Mooncake specific). Step 2: 21V shares RDP + shadow session ID. Connect via mstsc to mcpas.<region>.cloudapp.chinacloudapi.cn, then mstsc /shadow /control. Step 3: 21V engineer logs into jumpbox with CME credentials. Step 4: Access Everflow portal (bjs01n.netperf.chinacloudapi.cn for North, sha01n for East). Step 5: Configure capture filter JSON with SrcIp/DstIp pairs, submit rules, wait Synced. Step 6: Download traces via URL or everflow_download.ps1.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.13[MCVKB] [Tool sharing (Everflow)] how to exec/JIT Jumpbox for Overlake node capture.md

symptom: Need to find VM/VMSS TOR switch info, SLB ring details, and VPN Gateway container info for Everflow TOR-level packet capture setup
rootCause: TOR capture requires knowing the physical topology (T0 switch, port, node IP) which must be queried from multiple Kusto tables across different clusters
solution: VM T0 query: Join azurecm LogContainerSnapshot -> aznwmds.Servers (nodeId) -> DeviceInterfaceLinks (StartDevice/Port -> EndDevice=TOR) -> DeviceStatic (Cluster,Region) -> DeviceIpInterface (HostIP). SLB ring: azslbmc VipRangesSnapshotEvent (ipv4_is_in_range with VIP) for RingName. MUX PA: BgpPeerStateSnapshotEvent filter by Ring. VPNGW container: Geneva dashboard 35020946. Everflow portals: bjs01n/sha01n.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.13[MCVKB] [Tool sharing (Everflow)] how to exec/Tor capture notes.md

symptom: Mooncake JIT Jumpbox request not auto-approved anymore - requires manual approval, blocking access to Jarvis, ELK, and other troubleshooting tools
rootCause: Policy change in MC JIT portal: Escort-type RDP Jumpbox requests that were previously auto-approved now require manual approval. The email notification is sent only to the requester with no On-Call list included.
solution: Ask an available team peer to approve the JIT request. The approver SG is AME\AZURE-ALL-PSV (all AME users are members). Login MC JIT portal (jitaccess.security.core.chinacloudapi.cn), select Escort type + RDP (only Jumpbox). After approval, escort engineer shares shadow session credentials via Teams. Note: must use SAW or CORP VPN for the escort session.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.24 [MC Jump box] need the manually approval whe.md

symptom: Need to check when a Public IP (VIP) was allocated, deallocated, or changed - VIP lifecycle history needed for troubleshooting
rootCause: VIP lifecycle events (create, delete, reassign) are recorded in Kusto ResourceLifeCycleEvent table but not visible through Azure Portal
solution: Query Kusto endpoint aznwchinamc.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn: ResourceLifeCycleEvent | where Resource == "<VIP>" and TIMESTAMP > ago(30d). For deploymentID and cluster info of VIP, use Jarvis dashboard (ServiceId = deployment ID, FabricId = cluster).
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.7 [NET]how to check lifecycle of VIP.md

symptom: Need to inspect VFP stateful flow table on Azure host to debug datapath issues - cannot see active TCP/UDP flows from guest OS
rootCause: VFP stateful flow information is only accessible from the host level via FcShell, not visible from within the VM guest OS
solution: Use FcShell command: (Get-Fabric <ClusterID>).FabricConnection.NetworkManagementService.GetVfpFiltersForContainer("<NodeID>","<ContainerID>","<InterfaceMAC>","VFP_DEFAULT_LAYER_STATEFULL","","","","","","","list-flow","").VfpContainerConfiguration.VfpRules. Output shows IN/OUT flows with protocol, src:port => dst:port, type (allow/deny), flags, TTL, and pairing status.
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.3[Deprecated][NET]How to get the VFP stateful f.md

symptom: Need to find the TOR switch, NodeId, and other topology info for a specific VM using ContainerId
rootCause: VM physical topology (TOR, NodeIp) is not visible in Azure Portal; requires Kusto query against aznw networking tables
solution: Query aznw Kusto: database("aznwcosmos").NetworkVMAvailabilityCompleteV4_CAS | where ContainerId == "<id>" and TimeStamp > ago(5h) | join (AzServers | where TOR startswith "BJ" or TOR startswith "SH") on NodeIp | distinct NodeId, ContainerId, TenantId, LastKnownSubscriptionId, NodeIp, TOR. Note: filter TOR prefix by region (BJ=Beijing, SH=Shanghai for Mooncake).
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.4 [Deprecated]How to check TOR and other relate.md

symptom: Internal VNet packet loss or PingMesh availability issue - customer reports intermittent connectivity between VMs in same VNet or region
rootCause: Node-level network issue on the Azure host where the VM is running, causing internal VNet packet drops
solution: Use netvma tool (netvma.westcentralus.cloudapp.azure.com) to check internal VNet packet loss and PingMesh availability for the VM. If node-level issue is confirmed, advise customer to redeploy/resize VM to migrate it to a different healthy node
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/10.8[Deprecated] [NET]how to check internal Vnet p.md

symptom: Need to list all VMs running under a specific TOR (Top of Rack) switch for network troubleshooting - identifying impacted scope of TOR-level network issues
rootCause: TOR switch failure or degradation affecting multiple VMs - need to enumerate all VMs behind a given TOR to assess blast radius
solution: Query Kusto aznwcosmos database: join AzServers (filter by TOR name) with NetworkVMAvailabilityCompleteV4_CAS (filter by DataCenter prefix and recent TimeStamp) to get NodeId, NodeIp, ContainerId, TenantId, LastKnownSubscriptionId for all VMs under that TOR
sourceRef: MCVKB/Net/=======10.Tools=======/[Retired]10.6 List all VMs under specified TOR.md

symptom: Customer fails to access Azure Blob storage - timeout, 403 forbidden, or intermittent connectivity when accessing blob via private endpoint or public endpoint
rootCause: Multiple causes: (1) Connectivity - private endpoint misconfigured, NSG/firewall blocking; (2) Permission - storage access policy blocking, SAS token insufficient/expired; (3) Internal platform errors
solution: Step 1: Check ASC for failed transaction logs - if requests appear, connectivity OK, investigate HTTP code. If no requests, network issue. Step 2: Check storage networking config (PE, VNet restrictions). Step 3: Check access policies and SAS tokens. Step 4: For internal errors, engage PG
sourceRef: MCVKB/Critsit Handbook/VM+SCIM/Storage/1. Failed to access Blob.md

symptom: Cross-region connectivity failure between Global Azure and Mooncake - IPsec VPN not established, PaaS endpoint unreachable, or service hosted on Mooncake inaccessible from Global Azure
rootCause: Traffic blocked by GFW (Great Firewall) between Global Azure and China Azure, or routing issue on WAN edge devices
solution: For inbound (Global to China): (1) Use get-deploymentkusto.ps1 to find source region/Node/TOR info; (2) Use netperf.osdinfra.net Cluster Topology to find WAN device; (3) Use WAN Dashboard (wanedge.trafficmanager.net/LookingGlass) traceroute to identify traffic path and GFW location. For outbound (China to Global): traceroute not supported on WAN edge, use Netflow for blocking scenarios
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.15[NET] how to check the traceroute result and.md

symptom: Cannot see inner packet payload in Azure host network trace (ETL file) - Vxlan encapsulation hides actual VM traffic (CA addresses) in Netmon, cannot filter by customer IP or correlate with VFP rules
rootCause: Default Netmon cannot decode Vxlan encapsulation (Azure uses port 65330). ETL capture from Azure host contains Vxlan-encapsulated packets where VM traffic (CA IPs) is hidden inside the tunnel
solution: Method 1 (Wireshark): Convert ETL to CAP, configure Wireshark Vxlan decode on port 65330, filter by CA IP. Method 2 (Internal Netmon): Use internal Netmon with custom NPL files (vxlan.npl plus VFP trace NPL), update udp.npl and sparser.npl, rebuild profile. Capture command: netsh trace start provider=Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VfpExt provider=Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-VmSwitch capture=yes capturetype=both capturemultilayer=yes
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/7.11[MCVKB] [netmon]  How to decode the Vxlan payl.md

symptom: ASR A2A replication with private endpoint in Mooncake fails - DNS resolution failure for siterecovery endpoints when VM uses on-prem DNS server and /etc/hosts entries
rootCause: In Mooncake, siterecovery privatelink DNS lacks CNAME from service address to privatelink address. Mobility service queries privatelink address format directly, but customers configure /etc/hosts with service address format instead
solution: Configure /etc/hosts with privatelink addresses: change from xxx.bjb3.siterecovery.windowsazure.cn to xxx.bjb3.privatelink.siterecovery.windowsazure.cn. Also ensure all PE subnets have enough IP capacity since ASR re-creates all PE connections during protection
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/ASR (Deprecated)/## case study/[A2A][Private endpoint] cannot protect Linux VM.md

symptom: Deployment fails with RequestDisallowedByPolicy when creating public IP, dual NIC, NSG, or classic resources on subscription with corpnet ExpressRoute - SDOStdPolicyNetwork blocks the operation
rootCause: MSIT ISRM policy (SDOStdPolicyNetwork/SDOStdPolicyERNetworkRGV1) auto-applied to internal subscriptions with corpnet ExpressRoute. Prohibits public IP, dual NICs, classic resources, NSGs in ERNETWORK RGs to prevent corpnet resources from being publicly accessible
solution: CSS cannot modify these policies. Options: (1) Modify deployments to comply with ISRM rules; (2) Deploy to different subscription without corpnet ER; (3) Remove ER connection; (4) Contact MSIT SDO team via myexpressroute.azurewebsites.net for exceptions
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Policy and Management Group/== need refine ==/Scenarios/SDOStdPolicyNetwork and SDOStdPolicyERNetworkRGV1.md

symptom: Jarvis VNet get operation (RNM/Vnet management) returns access denied or locked in Mooncake - previously the get operation was unrestricted but now requires Escort session, blocking VNet troubleshooting
rootCause: Permission change in Mooncake: VNet get operation in Jarvis action now requires JIT Escort session approval (previously only set operations were locked). Both MSFT and AME accounts affected. Public Azure endpoint still allows get without restriction
solution: Step 1: Submit JIT request at jitaccess.security.core.chinacloudapi.cn (MSFT account sufficient). Step 2: Wait for manual approval from 21V escort team. Step 3: Connect via shadow RDP (mstsc /prompt /f /v:mcpas.ChinaNorth2...). Step 4: Escort team connects jumpbox and logs into Jarvis with CME account. Step 5: Run RNM/Vnet management Jarvis action through the escort session to dump VNet info
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.16[NET] how to run Vnet get operation in for RN.md

symptom: Static S2S VPN tunnel breaks or drops a few minutes before the 60-minute Quick Mode SA expiration - tunnel flaps periodically at ~57-60 minute intervals
rootCause: Azure Static VPN Gateways perform soft rekeying before the Quick Mode SA lifetime expires. The rekey process initiates slightly before the 60-minute mark, which can cause a brief interruption if the on-premises device does not handle soft rekey properly
solution: Ensure on-premises VPN device supports and is configured for soft rekeying (CREATE_CHILD_SA exchange). If using IKEv2, the device with the lower SA lifetime initiates rekey at approximately half-lifetime + random jitter. Verify the device accepts CREATE_CHILD_SA messages from Azure. For persistent issues, consider migrating to dynamic (route-based) gateway
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/5.2[VPN] VPN FAQ.md

symptom: Cannot modify Local Network Gateway address spaces while ARM VPN Gateway connection exists - Azure returns error 400: A gateway connection with the gateway prevents this operation
rootCause: ARM VPN Gateway design limitation: local network gateway properties cannot be updated while an active connection object references it. The connection object locks the local network gateway resource
solution: Workaround: (1) Delete the Azure Gateway Connection (Remove-AzVirtualNetworkGatewayConnection). (2) Update the local network gateway with new address spaces (Set-AzLocalNetworkGateway). (3) Recreate the Azure Gateway Connection (New-AzVirtualNetworkGatewayConnection). Note: this causes brief VPN downtime during the operation
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/5.2[VPN] VPN FAQ.md

symptom: Need to run inbound VFP process-tuples to debug connectivity between VMs in VNet peering or through SLB - ASC does not have inbound process tuple feature for encapsulated traffic
rootCause: ASC VM test traffic tool only supports basic process tuples without encap (no IP-GRE encapsulation support). For VNet peering and SLB inbound scenarios, traffic is GRE-encapsulated and requires manual process tuple construction with correct GRE key
solution: Format: "encap 1 [SourcePA] [DestPA] [GREKey] [6|17] [SrcIP] [SrcPort] [DstIP] [DstPort] [in|out] 1". For VNet peering: GRE key = source VNet GRE key (from Jarvis RNM GetVirtualNetworkSnapshotJSON). For SLB: GRE key = 0x100, Source PA = any MUX node IP from SLB ring. Run via Jarvis Action SupportabilityFabric/GetVfpFiltersForContainer or ARR script on VSAW. Check output for DECAP rules, NSG match, and NTSTATUS (0xc000009a = no underlay traffic)
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.17[NET]How to run Inbound process tuple under V.md

symptom: Need to capture packets on Azure Firewall instance for troubleshooting in Mooncake - standard portal packet capture not available or insufficient
rootCause: Azure Firewall internal packet capture requires Jarvis action with JIT Escort session in Mooncake. Default capture limit is only 1000 packets which may be insufficient for debugging
solution: Step 1: Apply Escort JIT (see 10.16 VNet get operation procedure). Step 2: In Jarvis action, request JIT access for AZFW capture. Step 3: Set Number of packets parameter higher than default 1000. Step 4: Run capture, copy file path from output. Step 5: Use Jarvis retrieval action with file path to get download links. Step 6: Download pcap files (use SAW machine if authorization error from corp machine). Also available for VPNGW and ERGW via separate Jarvis action links
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.18[Net]How to run packet capture on AZFW instan.md

symptom: Cross-region connectivity blocked by ISP/GFW between Azure China and public Internet or Global Azure - need to verify whether packets are being dropped at ISP/GFW level on Mooncake core routes
rootCause: Traffic between Mooncake and global Internet traverses ISP/GFW which may block certain protocols (e.g. UDP 500 for VPN). DDOS dashboard unavailable for Mooncake, making traffic pattern analysis difficult without core route netflow logs
solution: Use ELK (Elasticsearch/Kibana) on Mooncake core routes via Escort JIT session: (1) Apply Escort JIT for jumpbox (must use AZUTLA05/06 named jumpbox, not VMSS-based). (2) Login ELK at 10.43.15.234:5601 with ms-guest account. (3) Select ISP-specific template based on traceroute results. (4) Compare outbound vs inbound flow logs - if outbound packets present but no inbound response, traffic blocked by ISP/GFW. ELK stores 6 months of everflow data. Do NOT run multiple filter tasks simultaneously
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.19 [NET] How to run the flow logs (ELK) on core.md

symptom: Need to capture host-level network trace (VFP/VMSwitch) on Azure physical node in Mooncake for deep packet inspection - standard VM-level capture insufficient for diagnosing platform-level network issues
rootCause: Host-level packet capture requires direct RDP access to Azure physical node, which in Mooncake requires two-tier JIT approval: first JIT for jumpbox, then second JIT (with 21V CME account) for target node access
solution: Step 1: Get VM NodeId via ARR/ASC. Step 2: Apply first Escort JIT for jumpbox (MSFT account). Step 3: From jumpbox, apply second JIT at jitaccess.security.core.chinacloudapi.cn with 21V escort CME account for target node. Step 4: RDP to node via RDCMAN, create folder on D: drive. Step 5: Run capture: netsh trace start provider=Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Vmswitch capture=yes capturetype=both provider=Microsoft-Windows-Hyper-V-Vfpext tracefile=.\capture.etl persistent=yes report=dis maxSize=2048 PacketTruncateBytes=300. Step 6: Stop (netsh trace stop), xcopy files to jumpbox via tsclient, then upload to local
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.21 [NET] how to run the packet capture on Azure.md

symptom: Need to find the underlying containerID, nodeID, and host-level info for Azure SQL DB or MySQL PaaS instance for network troubleshooting - cannot directly see infrastructure details from PaaS resource
rootCause: Azure SQL DB and MySQL PaaS instances run on internal VMSS with SLB frontend. The mapping from PaaS endpoint to underlying container requires multi-step Kusto queries through SQL monitoring tables and SLB configuration
solution: Step 1: Query MonLogin table (sqlazurechi2.chinanorth2.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/MoonCake) to find server_name and NodeName for the DB. Step 2: Use psping to find SLB VIP from server FQDN. Step 3: Run get-azureip.ps1 -ipaddr <VIP> -slbdetails to get DIP-to-CA mapping, containerID, nodeID, cluster info. Step 4: Run get-deploymentkusto.ps1 -containerId <id> -cloudenv Mooncake -dashboards VMD,VFP,ASI to get performance and network dashboards. Alternative: use CADDAILY Kusto table with containerID to verify roleInstanceName mapping
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======Net======/10.22 How to find out the containerid_nodeid for A.md

symptom: Mooncake CDN management portal operation fails - HTTPS certificate binding, custom domain configuration, or other CDN operations return errors without clear error details in portal
rootCause: CDN management operations go through CDN REST API (restapi.cdn.azure.cn). Failures like certificate-domain mismatch (CertificateAndEndpointNotMatch) or other API errors are not always surfaced clearly in portal UI
solution: Use Jarvis to query CDN operation errors: Endpoint=CA Mooncake, Namespace=MCCDNPortal, Events=ApplicationEvents, Scoping=UnifiedPortalWebRoleV2, Filter=AnyField contains <SubscriptionID>. Then add client query: where EventData.containsi('<custom domain>') and Level == '2' (Error level). EventData will show the full API request/response including detailed error messages. Common error: CertificateAndEndpointNotMatch when certificate domain doesn't match endpoint custom domain
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======17. CDN=======/17.1 [CDN] Jarvis for Mooncake CDN troubleshooting.md

symptom: Cannot create CDN profile in Azure China (Mooncake) portal - error 'Oops, could not create the marketplace item' when trying to create CDN profile from marketplace
rootCause: Known platform bug in Mooncake CDN marketplace item. The direct marketplace creation path for CDN profile is broken. PG is working on the fix
solution: Workaround: Do not create CDN profile from marketplace directly. Instead, go to portal -> Create Resource -> search 'CDN' -> select CDN and create from there. This alternative creation path bypasses the broken marketplace item
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======17. CDN=======/17.2 无法创建CDN profile，could not create the marketpl.md

symptom: Cannot delete subnet or VNet after ACI (Azure Container Instances) is deleted - error states subnet requires delegation to Microsoft.ContainerInstance/containerGroups due to service association link
rootCause: When ACI is deployed into a VNet subnet, it creates a Service Association Link (SAL) and network profile with subnet delegation. Deleting the ACI container group does not automatically clean up the SAL, network profile, and delegation, leaving a dangling reference that blocks subnet/VNet deletion
solution: Clean up in order: (1) Delete network profile: az network profile list --resource-group <RG> then az network profile delete --id <PROFILE_ID> -y. (2) Delete SAL: construct SAL ID as <subnet-id>/providers/Microsoft.ContainerInstance/serviceAssociationLinks/default, then az resource delete --ids <SAL_ID> --api-version <latest>. (3) Remove subnet delegation: az network vnet subnet update --resource-group <RG> --vnet-name <VNET> --name <SUBNET> --remove delegations. (4) Now delete subnet and VNet normally
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/ACI/##Case Study/1. cannot delete subnet after ACI deleted.md
